title: Cerasus glabra
taxonomy: Cerasus glabra is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, Rosaceae. It is a type of deciduous shrub native to East Asia. This plant belongs to the genus Cerasus, which is part of the subgenus Cerasus. The genus Cerasus is often divided into two subgenera: Cerasus and Padus. Cerasus glabra's classification within the subgenus is not specified.
uses: Cerasus glabra, also known as the European bird cherry, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The bark and leaves of the plant contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In folk medicine, Cerasus glabra has been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antiviral effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Cerasus glabra.

parts: Cerasus glabra, commonly known as the rough-leaved cherry or weeping cherry, is a species of flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. preparations: Cerasus glabra, also known as the downy cherry, is a species of cherry that has been used in traditional herbal medicine for various purposes.
